name: Cloudflare Workers MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Cloudflare Workers MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Cloudflare's developer platform — deploying and managing Workers functions, configuring Cloudflare services (DNS, CDN, security rules), querying D1 databases, managing KV stores, and integrating Cloudflare's edge computing infrastructure into agent-driven DevOps and serverless deployment workflows.
use_cases: Deploying and managing Cloudflare Workers from DevOps automation agents
Querying Cloudflare D1 database from application management agents
Managing KV store data from application configuration agents
Configuring Cloudflare security and DNS settings from infrastructure agents
Building serverless deployment pipelines from CI/CD agents
Monitoring Cloudflare analytics and performance from observability agents
not_for: AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, or other serverless platforms (use their MCPs)
best_when: A developer deploying on Cloudflare Workers needs AI-assisted deployment, configuration, and management of their edge computing infrastructure.
avoid_when: You deploy on AWS, GCP, or other cloud platforms — or don't use Cloudflare.

auth.notes: Cloudflare API token required. Generate from Cloudflare dashboard with specific permissions. Granular permissions available: Workers write, D1 read/write, DNS write, etc. Use least-privilege token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Cloudflare Workers: 100K requests/day free; D1: 5GB storage free
pricing.notes: Cloudflare Workers free tier is generous. Paid plan for higher throughput. Official MCP from Cloudflare.

performance.rate_limits: Cloudflare API: 1,200 requests/5 minutes per token

known_agent_gotchas: Account ID required for most operations — find in Cloudflare dashboard right sidebar
API tokens are permission-specific — create token with only required permissions
Worker deployments are live immediately — test with staging environments first
Wrangler CLI (wasm-based) may be needed alongside API for some operations
Official Cloudflare MCP — significantly higher quality than community alternatives
D1 SQL is SQLite-compatible — slight dialect differences from PostgreSQL/MySQL
agent_readiness.security_notes: API token with granular permissions. HTTPS enforced. SOC2/GDPR/ISO27001. Official Cloudflare MCP. Least-privilege token. Deployments affect live infrastructure.

security_score_components.security_notes: Granular API tokens. HTTPS. SOC2/GDPR. Official Cloudflare. Least-privilege. Live infra impact.
